BP-1.name: Discarded Error Return
BP-1.description: A returned error is assigned to `_`, suppressing error handling.
BP-1.detection_notes: Match assignment_statement or short_var_declaration with `_ =` on an error-producing call.

BP-2.name: Naked Error Return
BP-2.description: An error is returned without wrapping contextual information.
BP-2.detection_notes: Match `return err` sites that lose operation-specific context.

BP-3.name: Panic Outside Main Or Test
BP-3.description: panic is called outside main() or test files.
BP-3.detection_notes: Match panic() outside main() and `_test.go` files.

BP-4.name: Recover Without Logging
BP-4.description: recover() is used without recording the recovered panic.
BP-4.detection_notes: Match recover() handlers that do not log, report, or rethrow the panic.

BP-5.name: Ignored Close Error
BP-5.description: Close() is called without checking its returned error.
BP-5.detection_notes: Match `.Close()` calls whose error result is ignored.

BP-6.name: WaitGroup Add Inside Goroutine
BP-6.description: sync.WaitGroup.Add is called inside the goroutine it tracks.
BP-6.detection_notes: Review-only AST match for `go func` bodies that call `.Add(`; nested goroutines are scoped independently, but receiver type is not proven and atomic counters can match.

BP-7.name: Mutex Passed By Value
BP-7.description: sync.Mutex is passed by value, copying lock state.
BP-7.detection_notes: Match function parameters that take `sync.Mutex` by value.

BP-8.name: Unlock Deferred On Mutex Copy
BP-8.description: defer mu.Unlock() is used on a mutex value copy.
BP-8.detection_notes: Match deferred unlocks on mutex values that have been copied.

BP-9.name: Blocking Select Without Timeout
BP-9.description: select waits without a default branch, timeout, or context cancellation.
BP-9.detection_notes: Match `select {}` blocks with no escape hatch.

BP-10.name: time.After Inside Loop
BP-10.description: time.After is called inside a loop, allocating a timer per iteration.

BP-11.name: Defer Inside Loop
BP-11.description: defer is used inside a loop body.
BP-11.detection_notes: Match defer statements nested in loop bodies.

BP-12.name: Unbuffered Channel Send From Multiple Goroutines
BP-12.description: Multiple goroutines send on an unbuffered channel without coordinated receivers.
BP-12.detection_notes: Review-only same-file heuristic; it cannot prove channel ownership, all receiver paths, or helper-mediated coordination.

BP-13.name: Background Context In Library Function
BP-13.description: context.Background() is used outside main or initialization code.
BP-13.detection_notes: Match `context.Background()` in library functions and helper code.

BP-14.name: Goroutine Without Context Cancellation
BP-14.description: A goroutine is launched without listening for ctx.Done() or another shutdown signal.
BP-14.detection_notes: Review-only same-file heuristic; it cannot prove ownership, alternative shutdown channels, or helper-mediated lifecycle control.

BP-15.name: Recursive sync.Once.Do
BP-15.description: sync.Once.Do invokes a closure that recursively calls the same Once.
BP-15.detection_notes: Match direct or same-file helper call chains that recurse into the same `sync.Once.Do`.

BP-16.name: time.Sleep In Test
BP-16.description: A test uses time.Sleep instead of deterministic synchronization or polling.
BP-16.detection_notes: Match `time.Sleep()` in `_test.go` files outside controlled retry loops.

BP-17.name: Redundant t.Error Before t.Fatal
BP-17.description: A test calls t.Error and then immediately escalates to t.Fatal.
BP-17.detection_notes: Match consecutive `t.Error` or `t.Errorf` followed by `t.Fatal` or `t.Fatalf`.

BP-18.name: t.Error Without Early Exit
BP-18.description: A test records an error and continues execution without returning or failing now.
BP-18.detection_notes: Match `t.Error` or `t.Errorf` paths that keep executing the same block.

BP-19.name: Missing t.Helper In Test Helper
BP-19.description: A helper used by tests omits t.Helper(), reducing useful failure locations.
BP-19.detection_notes: Match helper functions called from tests whose first statement is not `t.Helper()`.

BP-20.name: Table Test Without t.Run
BP-20.description: A table-driven test loops over cases without subtests.
BP-20.detection_notes: Match table-driven loops in `_test.go` files that do not call `t.Run`.

BP-21.name: Subtest Missing t.Parallel
BP-21.description: A table-driven subtest omits t.Parallel where the pattern expects isolated parallel cases.
BP-21.detection_notes: Match `t.Run` bodies in table tests that omit `t.Parallel()`.

BP-22.name: TestMain Without os.Exit
BP-22.description: TestMain does not exit with the result of m.Run().

BP-23.name: Long Test Without testing.Short Guard
BP-23.description: A long-running test does not respect testing.Short().
BP-23.detection_notes: Heuristic for large `_test.go` bodies with no `testing.Short()` guard.

BP-24.name: Test File Without Test Functions
BP-24.detection_notes: Match `_test.go` files with zero `func Test*` declarations.

BP-25.name: Test Helper Returns Error Instead Of Failing Test
BP-25.description: A test helper returns an error that the caller always converts into t.Fatal-style handling.
BP-25.detection_notes: Match helper functions in tests that return `error` instead of owning the test failure path.

BP-26.name: Context Not First Parameter
BP-26.description: A public Go API accepts context.Context but not as its first parameter.
BP-26.detection_notes: Match parameter lists where `context.Context` is present but not first.

BP-27.name: Exported Function Returns Unexported Type
BP-27.description: An exported function returns a package-private concrete type.
BP-27.detection_notes: Match exported functions whose return types start with a lowercase identifier.

BP-28.name: Single Method Interface
BP-28.description: An interface with one method could often be represented as a function type.
BP-28.detection_notes: Match interface declarations with exactly one method.

BP-29.name: Interface Bloat
BP-29.description: An interface declares too many methods and becomes difficult to satisfy or mock.
BP-29.detection_notes: Match interfaces with more than five methods.

BP-30.name: Exported Interface Without Documented Implementation
BP-30.description: An exported interface is exposed without clear evidence of a same-package implementation; this is opt-in style advice because external implementations are common.
BP-30.detection_notes: Match exported interfaces whose implementing types are not evident in the package. Review external implementations and intentional capability boundaries manually.

BP-31.name: Function Returns Concrete Type Instead Of Interface
BP-31.description: A function exposes a concrete implementation where an interface boundary would be more stable.
BP-31.detection_notes: Heuristic for exported constructors or factories returning concrete implementation types.

BP-32.name: String Alias Error Type
BP-32.description: An error type is modeled as a string alias instead of a structured type.
BP-32.detection_notes: Match `type X string` definitions that implement `Error() string`.

BP-33.name: Sentinel Error Without Is Method
BP-33.description: A sentinel-like error type lacks an Is method for stable comparisons.
BP-33.detection_notes: Match custom error types intended as sentinels without `Is(error) bool`.

BP-34.name: Error Wrapping Without Percent-W
BP-34.description: fmt.Errorf wraps an error using `%v` or `%s` instead of `%w`.
BP-34.detection_notes: Match `fmt.Errorf` calls that mention an error argument without `%w`.

BP-36.name: init With Side Effects
BP-36.description: An init() function performs work beyond simple variable setup.
BP-36.detection_notes: Match `init()` bodies containing non-trivial side-effect statements.

BP-37.name: Package-Level Mutable Global
BP-37.description: A package exposes mutable global state outside tests.
BP-37.detection_notes: Match package-level mutable variables in non-test files.

BP-38.name: Unused Unexported Helper
BP-38.description: An unexported helper has no same-package callers.
BP-38.detection_notes: Match unexported helper functions with zero internal call sites.

BP-39.name: Exported Function Without Doc Comment
BP-39.description: An exported API surface is missing its doc comment.
BP-39.detection_notes: Match exported functions without preceding package comments.

BP-40.name: Unrelated Constants In One Block
BP-40.description: A package-level const block groups unrelated constants together.
BP-40.detection_notes: Heuristic for const blocks whose names do not share a prefix or domain.

BP-41.name: Missing Package Doc Comment
BP-41.description: A package lacks a package-level documentation comment.
BP-41.detection_notes: Match Go files in a package set with no package doc comment.

BP-42.name: One-Off Import Alias
BP-42.description: An import alias is introduced without consistent package-wide benefit.
BP-42.detection_notes: Match aliased imports that appear only once or without repeated local use.

BP-43.name: Dot Import Outside Tests
BP-43.description: A dot import is used in production code.

BP-44.name: Blank Import Without Justification
BP-44.description: A blank import appears outside conventional driver or image registration cases.
BP-44.detection_notes: Match blank imports without a nearby justification or standard registration pattern.

BP-45.name: Inconsistent Receiver Name
BP-45.description: Methods on the same receiver type use inconsistent receiver names.
BP-45.detection_notes: Match receiver-name drift across methods on the same type.

BP-46.name: HTTP Server Without Timeouts
BP-46.description: An HTTP server is configured without read or write timeouts.

BP-47.name: Missing Graceful Shutdown
BP-47.description: A long-running server starts without a graceful shutdown path.
BP-47.detection_notes: Match server startup code with no `Shutdown` handling.

BP-48.name: log.Fatal Or os.Exit In Library Code
BP-48.description: A non-main function exits the process directly.
BP-48.detection_notes: Match `log.Fatal*` or `os.Exit` outside main/test entry points.

BP-49.name: Deferred Cleanup Without Error Handling
BP-49.description: A deferred cleanup drops an error that can affect correctness.
BP-49.detection_notes: Match deferred functions that ignore cleanup errors.

BP-50.name: Missing Signal Handling In Long-Running Process
BP-50.description: A long-running process does not watch SIGTERM or SIGINT for shutdown.
BP-50.detection_notes: Match service-style main functions with no signal handling.

BP-51.name: Recover Without Re-Panic In Library Code
BP-51.description: Library code recovers a panic and continues without preserving failure semantics.
BP-51.detection_notes: Match recover handlers in library code that suppress the panic entirely.

BP-52.name: Unchecked Integer Multiplication
BP-52.description: Arithmetic multiplies values without obvious overflow bounds checks.
BP-52.detection_notes: Heuristic for multiplication-heavy code with no guard or size validation.

BP-53.name: Gob Registration Mismatch
BP-53.description: encoding/gob registration appears inconsistent with the encoded types.
BP-53.detection_notes: Match gob.Register patterns that do not line up with later encoder or decoder usage.

BP-54.name: Public HTTP Endpoint Without Rate Limiting
BP-54.description: A public HTTP endpoint is exposed without any rate-limiting guard.
BP-54.detection_notes: Heuristic for public router setup with no rate-limiter middleware.

BP-55.name: Missing Request ID Propagation
BP-55.description: Middleware chains do not propagate a request identifier through request handling.
BP-55.detection_notes: Match middleware stacks that log or branch on requests without request-id propagation.

BP-56.name: Deprecated Standard Library Package
BP-56.description: Code imports a deprecated package or legacy context path.

BP-57.name: Stale Go Version In go.mod
BP-57.description: go.mod targets a Go version materially behind the current supported baseline.
BP-57.detection_notes: Project-level go.mod parse comparing the declared Go version with policy.

BP-58.name: Unpinned Dependency Version
BP-58.description: A dependency declaration is too loose to be reproducible.
BP-58.detection_notes: Project-level go.mod parse looking for non-specific versions.

BP-59.name: Unused Direct Dependency
BP-59.description: A direct dependency is listed but not imported anywhere in the project.

BP-60.name: Test Dependency In Main Module
BP-60.description: A dependency only needed for tests lives in the main module dependency set.
BP-60.detection_notes: Project-level analysis of go.mod usage versus test-only imports.

BP-61.name: Missing Indirect Annotation
BP-61.description: An indirect dependency is present without the expected `// indirect` marker.
BP-61.detection_notes: Project-level go.mod parse comparing the dependency graph with annotations.

BP-62.name: Dependency Used In One File
BP-62.description: A dependency is only used once and may not justify an external module.
BP-62.detection_notes: Project-wide import frequency heuristic for low-use dependencies.

BP-63.name: Dependency With Known CVE Not Updated
BP-63.description: A dependency version remains on a release associated with a known vulnerability.
BP-63.detection_notes: Reserved for dependency metadata and vulnerability feed integration.

BP-64.name: Local Filesystem Replace Directive
BP-64.description: go.mod contains a replace directive that points at the local filesystem.
BP-64.detection_notes: Project-level go.mod parse for `replace` directives with local paths.

BP-65.name: Missing go.sum Entries
BP-65.description: A module references dependencies without complete go.sum coverage.
BP-65.detection_notes: Project-level verification that go.mod and go.sum stay in sync.

BP-66.name: Wrapped Sentinel Compared Directly
BP-66.description: A wrapped sentinel error is compared with == or !=, bypassing the wrapped error chain.
BP-66.detection_notes: Match same-function fmt.Errorf calls with %w around an Err-like sentinel followed by direct comparison of the error value; use errors.Is instead.

BP-67.name: errors.As Target Not Passed By Address
BP-67.description: errors.As receives a target value instead of an addressable pointer and can panic at runtime.
BP-67.detection_notes: Match errors.As calls whose second argument is not visibly address-prefixed; keep the rule limited to the stdlib errors.As call shape.

BP-68.name: Discarded errors.Join Result
BP-68.description: The combined error returned by errors.Join is discarded, losing the joined failure information.
BP-68.detection_notes: Match stdlib errors.Join calls used as expression statements or assigned only to `_`; do not duplicate ordinary discarded-error assignment findings.

BP-72.name: Typed Nil Interface Return
BP-72.description: A typed nil pointer is returned through an error or interface result, producing a non-nil interface value.
BP-72.detection_notes: Match `var p *T = nil` followed by direct `return p` from a visible error or anonymous interface result.

BP-73.name: Nil Map Write Without Initialization
BP-73.description: A function-local map is indexed before it is initialized with make, which will panic at runtime.
BP-73.detection_notes: Match a local `var name map[...]` declaration followed by an index assignment in the same function with no visible make assignment before the write.

BP-75.name: Copy Into Zero-Length Slice
BP-75.description: copy writes into a statically zero-length destination, so no source element can be copied.
BP-75.detection_notes: Match copy calls whose destination is a local zero-value or make(..., 0) slice and whose source is a non-empty slice literal.

BP-76.name: Map Range Used For Ordered Output
BP-76.description: Values collected directly from map iteration are used as ordered output even though map iteration is nondeterministic.
BP-76.detection_notes: Match a local map ranged into a slice that is later passed to strings.Join without an intervening sort call.

BP-79.name: Context Cancellation Not Released
BP-79.description: A locally bound context cancellation function has no visible call or defer in the same function.
BP-79.detection_notes: Review-only heuristic: match WithCancel, WithTimeout, or WithDeadline bindings whose cancel identifier is never visibly called or deferred locally.

BP-80.name: context.TODO In Production Code
BP-80.description: context.TODO leaves production context ownership unresolved outside test files.
BP-80.detection_notes: Match exact context.TODO calls outside test files; do not treat this as a proof of a runtime defect.

BP-81.name: Repeated time.Now In Condition
BP-81.description: A condition reads the clock more than once and can compare values from different instants.
BP-81.detection_notes: Match multiple time.Now calls in one boolean condition; avoid standalone timestamp assignments.

BP-84.name: Integer Percentage Truncation
BP-84.description: Integer division occurs before multiplication by 100 in a percentage-shaped calculation.
BP-84.detection_notes: Match simple `a / b * 100` expressions only when the destination or function name visibly indicates a percentage.

BP-85.name: Unchecked Request Context Assertion
BP-85.description: A net/http handler asserts a request context value without checking whether the value exists and has the expected type.
BP-85.detection_notes: Match single-value Context.Value type assertions inside functions with typed http.ResponseWriter and *http.Request parameters.

BP-86.name: Mutex Lock Without Unlock
BP-86.description: A locally declared mutex is locked without a visible matching Unlock in the same function.
BP-86.detection_notes: Match sync.Mutex or sync.RWMutex receivers declared locally or as pointer parameters with Lock and no same-receiver Unlock; branch completeness is out of scope.

BP-87.name: RLock Held Across Blocking Call
BP-87.description: A read lock remains held while an obvious blocking operation executes.
BP-87.detection_notes: Match a declared sync.RWMutex receiver with RLock, a blocking call or channel receive, and a later RUnlock in the same function.

BP-88.name: Nil Channel Send Or Receive
BP-88.description: A local zero-value channel is used directly outside a select and will block indefinitely.
BP-88.detection_notes: Match direct send/receive operations on a local var ch chan T before a visible make assignment; ignore nil-channel select cases.

BP-89.name: Repeated Unconditional Channel Close
BP-89.description: The same channel is closed more than once by unconditional statements in one function.
BP-89.detection_notes: Match repeated builtin close calls on the same simple identifier outside conditionals and loops; ownership and reachability beyond this local shape are out of scope.

BP-90.name: Channel Receive Loop Without Exit
BP-90.description: An infinite channel receive loop has no visible local cancellation or exit path.
BP-90.detection_notes: Match `for {}` loops containing a bare channel receive without select, break, or return.

BP-91.name: Data-Bearing Notification Channel
BP-91.description: A boolean or integer channel carries a constant notification value while receivers discard the value.
BP-91.detection_notes: Match notification-shaped channels that send constants and receive without using the value.

BP-92.name: errgroup Without Context
BP-92.description: An errgroup is used without the cancellation-aware WithContext form.
BP-92.detection_notes: Match locally declared errgroup.Group values used with Go or Wait and no errgroup.WithContext call.

BP-93.name: errgroup Closure Discards Error
BP-93.description: An errgroup closure explicitly discards an operation error instead of returning it to the group.
BP-93.detection_notes: Match blank assignment of an error-producing call inside an errgroup Go closure.

BP-94.name: Goroutine Map Write Without Sync
BP-94.description: A goroutine writes to a shared map without a visible synchronization boundary.
BP-94.detection_notes: Match a goroutine map-index assignment in a function with multiple goroutines and no visible mutex or channel handoff.

BP-96.name: sql.Rows Without Close
BP-96.description: A database rows value is acquired without a visible Close call.
BP-96.detection_notes: Match Query or QueryContext results assigned to a rows-like local without a later Close in the same function.

BP-97.name: Writer Not Flushed Before Read
BP-97.description: A buffered or compressed writer is read through its underlying buffer before being flushed.
BP-97.detection_notes: Match writes through bufio or gzip writers followed by a read of the underlying buffer without Flush or Close.

BP-98.name: Opened File Without Close Or Transfer
BP-98.description: An os.Open or os.OpenFile result is neither closed nor visibly transferred to the caller.
BP-98.detection_notes: Review-only same-function heuristic for local os.Open/OpenFile bindings with no visible Close or return transfer.

BP-99.name: Cond Wait Without Locker
BP-99.description: sync.Cond.Wait is used without a visible Lock or RLock on its associated locker.
BP-99.detection_notes: Match locally created sync.Cond values whose same-function body has Wait but no visible Lock/RLock on the constructor locker.

BP-100.name: Unbounded Goroutine Fan-Out
BP-100.description: A loop launches one goroutine per item without a visible concurrency bound.
BP-100.detection_notes: Match goroutine launches inside range loops without a semaphore, worker pool, or errgroup limit.

BP-101.name: HTTP Header Written After Body
BP-101.description: A net/http handler writes a response body before setting its intended status header.
BP-101.detection_notes: Match net/http handler functions with ResponseWriter and Request parameters where a writer body call precedes WriteHeader in the same block.

BP-102.name: HTTP Error Path Without Response
BP-102.description: A net/http handler returns from an error path without writing an error response or status.
BP-102.detection_notes: Match typed net/http handlers with a local err != nil guard and direct bare return when the guarded branch has no response action.

BP-104.name: Duplicate ServeMux Pattern
BP-104.description: The same literal pattern is registered more than once on a net/http ServeMux.
BP-104.detection_notes: Match repeated literal Handle or HandleFunc registrations on a locally created http.ServeMux.

BP-105.name: Sensitive Cookie Missing Security Flags
BP-105.description: A sensitive cookie is issued without Secure and HttpOnly protections.
BP-105.detection_notes: Match sensitive cookie literals in net/http handlers missing either Secure or HttpOnly.

BP-107.name: HTTP Middleware Missing Next
BP-107.description: A net/http middleware returns a handler that neither delegates to the next handler nor writes a terminal response.
BP-107.detection_notes: Match typed net/http middleware closures with no next call and no explicit response action.

BP-109.name: Gin Error Response Without Abort
BP-109.description: A Gin handler writes an error response and continues without aborting or returning.
BP-109.detection_notes: Match imported Gin handlers with typed *gin.Context parameters where an error JSON response is not followed by Abort or return in its block.

BP-110.name: Gin Bind Error Ignored
BP-110.description: A Gin binding call is used as a bare statement and its returned error is discarded.
BP-110.detection_notes: Match imported Gin handlers with typed *gin.Context parameters where ShouldBind or ShouldBindJSON is a bare expression statement.

BP-116.name: Echo Error Response And Raw Error
BP-116.description: An Echo handler writes an error response and returns the raw error, creating two response-handling paths.
BP-116.detection_notes: Match imported Echo handlers with typed echo.Context parameters where an error JSON response is followed by a raw error return.

BP-117.name: Echo Bind Error Ignored
BP-117.description: An Echo binding call is used as a bare statement and its returned error is discarded.
BP-117.detection_notes: Match imported Echo handlers with typed echo.Context parameters where Bind is a bare expression statement.

BP-120.name: Fiber BodyParser Error Ignored
BP-120.description: A Fiber body parser is used as a bare statement and its returned error is discarded.
BP-120.detection_notes: Match imported Fiber handlers with typed *fiber.Ctx parameters where BodyParser is a bare expression statement.

BP-122.name: Chi Middleware Missing Next
BP-122.description: A Chi middleware returns without calling the next handler or writing a terminal response.
BP-122.detection_notes: Match imported Chi middleware closures with no next.ServeHTTP call and no explicit response action.

BP-128.name: QueryRow Scan Without ErrNoRows Handling
BP-128.description: QueryRow.Scan errors are handled without distinguishing sql.ErrNoRows.
BP-128.detection_notes: Match typed database/sql QueryRow calls whose Scan error path has no ErrNoRows handling.

BP-131.name: Query Used For DML Without Rows
BP-131.description: database/sql Query is used for a literal DML statement that does not return rows.
BP-131.detection_notes: Review-only import-gated heuristic for literal INSERT, UPDATE, or DELETE passed to Query or QueryContext without RETURNING.

BP-132.name: Update Without RowsAffected Check
BP-132.description: An optimistic-lock-shaped update ignores RowsAffected and cannot detect a lost update.
BP-132.detection_notes: Match UPDATE execution with a version predicate and no visible RowsAffected check.

BP-133.name: GORM Chain Error Ignored
BP-133.description: A GORM chain result is used without checking its Error field.
BP-133.detection_notes: Match typed GORM chains whose result is consumed without a visible Error check.

BP-134.name: GORM First Without Not Found Handling
BP-134.description: A GORM First or Take query does not distinguish gorm.ErrRecordNotFound.
BP-134.detection_notes: Match typed GORM First or Take error paths with no ErrRecordNotFound handling.

BP-135.name: GORM Global Without Session
BP-135.description: A package-level GORM handle is chained directly in a request path without a session boundary.
BP-135.detection_notes: Match imported GORM package globals used in typed request handlers without Session or WithContext.

BP-136.name: GORM AutoMigrate In Request Path
BP-136.description: GORM schema migration is executed from a request handler instead of startup or a migration command.
BP-136.detection_notes: Match AutoMigrate on a typed *gorm.DB parameter in a function that also accepts net/http or recognized framework request parameters.

BP-138.name: External I/O In GORM Hook
BP-138.description: A GORM lifecycle hook performs direct external HTTP or SMTP I/O before the database operation is safely committed.
BP-138.detection_notes: Match recognized GORM hook methods with a *gorm.DB parameter and direct imported http or smtp calls; helper ownership is out of scope.

BP-140.name: sqlx Retrieval Error Ignored
BP-140.description: A sqlx StructScan or Get retrieval call is used as a bare expression and its error is discarded.
BP-140.detection_notes: Match imported sqlx retrieval calls used as expression statements.

BP-141.name: sqlx Named Placeholder Missing Struct Tag
BP-141.description: A sqlx named query uses a snake_case placeholder that an untagged Go field will not map to under the default mapper.
BP-141.detection_notes: Match typed sqlx named execution with a same-file struct/value binding, snake_case placeholders, and no matching db tag; custom mapper configuration is not inferred.

BP-142.name: sqlx.In Query Without Rebind
BP-142.description: A query expanded by sqlx.In is executed without rebinding placeholders for the database driver.
BP-142.detection_notes: Match same-function sqlx.In output reaching a typed *sqlx.DB or *sqlx.Tx execution call without an intervening receiver Rebind call.

BP-143.name: Redis Result Error Ignored
BP-143.description: A go-redis command result is used as a bare expression and its error is discarded.
BP-143.detection_notes: Match imported go-redis command calls used as expression statements without checking Result().Err().

BP-145.name: pgx Pool Connection Not Released
BP-145.description: A pgx pool connection is acquired without a visible Release or Close path.
BP-145.detection_notes: Review-only import-gated same-function heuristic for pgxpool Acquire results with no later Release or Close.

BP-146.name: Sensitive Fields Logged
BP-146.description: A logger receives a sensitive field or value without an obvious redaction step.
BP-146.detection_notes: Match sensitive field names or environment values passed to log, slog, or zap calls without redaction.

BP-147.name: Unstructured Service Logging
BP-147.description: A non-main service package mixes the standard logger with a structured logger.
BP-147.detection_notes: Match log.Print calls in non-main packages that also import log/slog or zap.

BP-149.name: Error Log Without Attribute
BP-149.description: An error-level logger call inside an error branch omits the error as a structured attribute.
BP-149.detection_notes: Match err != nil branches with error-level slog or zap calls that do not include err.

BP-151.name: Secret Environment Value Logged
BP-151.description: A sensitive environment value is passed directly to a logger and may expose a secret in logs.
BP-151.detection_notes: Match direct os.Getenv calls for secret-like names passed to stdlib log, log/slog, or explicit zap.L/zap.S logger calls.

BP-155.name: Unbounded JSON Request Body
BP-155.description: A JSON decoder reads an HTTP request body without a visible size limit.
BP-155.detection_notes: Match json.Decoder on http.Request.Body without http.MaxBytesReader or an equivalent bounded reader.

BP-156.name: Security-Sensitive JSON Omitempty
BP-156.description: A security-sensitive JSON field relies on omitempty and can silently disappear when zero-valued.
BP-156.detection_notes: Match sensitive struct fields with json omitempty tags in request or security-shaped types.

BP-159.name: Flag Value Read Before Parse
BP-159.description: A flag pointer is dereferenced before flag.Parse processes command-line arguments.
BP-159.detection_notes: Match flag pointer declarations and dereferences before the first flag.Parse call in the same function.

BP-161.name: Test Uses Production DSN
BP-161.description: A test opens a literal database target containing an explicit production marker.
BP-161.detection_notes: Match test files with imported database/sql or GORM calls whose literal sql.Open or gorm.Open text contains a standalone prod or production marker; local/container targets are ignored.

BP-162.name: Parallel Test Mutates Shared State
BP-162.description: A parallel test mutates package-level state that can race with other tests.
BP-162.detection_notes: Match Test, Benchmark, or Fuzz functions calling t.Parallel and assigning to package-level variables in the same test file.

BP-163.name: Golden Update Without Short Guard
BP-163.description: A golden-file update path writes output without skipping short tests.
BP-163.detection_notes: Match test update flags and golden writes in an update branch when the test function has no testing.Short call.

BP-164.name: Functional Option Mutates Global Default
BP-164.description: An exported functional option changes package-level default state instead of only configuring its supplied instance.
BP-164.detection_notes: Match exported With* functions returning an Option whose body assigns to a package-level variable.

BP-70.name: Logged Error Then Continued
BP-70.description: An error is logged but the error path continues without an explicit exit.
BP-70.detection_notes: Match a local `err != nil` branch containing an error logger and no bare return; do not infer the wider function contract.

BP-82.name: Time Parsed Without Location
BP-82.description: time.Parse uses the local default location when the application may require an explicit location.
BP-82.detection_notes: Match exact time.Parse calls outside test files; this is advisory and does not infer the application's timezone policy.

BP-83.name: Sleep Used For Synchronization
BP-83.description: time.Sleep is used as a synchronization boundary instead of explicit coordination.
BP-83.detection_notes: Match production time.Sleep calls while excluding obvious retry, backoff, jitter, and delay-shaped lines.

BP-95.name: HTTP Response Body Without Close
BP-95.description: An HTTP response body is acquired without a visible Close call.
BP-95.detection_notes: Match local client Do results without Body.Close; this provides zero-dependency coverage and overlaps bodyclose/sqlclosecheck.

BP-111.name: Gin Context In Goroutine
BP-111.description: A Gin context is used in a goroutine without a c.Copy lifetime boundary.
BP-111.detection_notes: Require the Gin import and match c. use inside a goroutine without Copy in the same local block; PERF overlap requires review.

BP-119.name: Fiber Context In Goroutine
BP-119.description: A Fiber context is captured in a goroutine without a visible lifetime boundary.
BP-119.detection_notes: Require the Fiber import and match c. or ctx. use inside a goroutine without Immutable or Copy; PERF overlap requires review.

BP-126.name: Transaction Without Completion
BP-126.description: A database transaction has no visible Commit or Rollback path.
BP-126.detection_notes: Match a local database/sql Begin or BeginTx call whose containing function has no visible Commit or Rollback; ownership transfer is out of scope.

BP-154.name: Discarded json.Unmarshal Error
BP-154.description: json.Unmarshal is used without handling its returned error.
BP-154.detection_notes: Match exact json.Unmarshal expression statements or blank assignments outside test files; document overlap with generic ignored-error rules.

BP-158.name: Naked gRPC Error Return
BP-158.description: A gRPC error is returned naked despite importing status helpers.
BP-158.detection_notes: Require google.golang.org/grpc/status and match return err lines when status helpers are present; do not infer service-wide error policy.

BP-160.name: Cobra Run Without RunE
BP-160.description: A Cobra command uses Run instead of RunE and may swallow command errors.
BP-160.detection_notes: Require the Cobra import and match command literals with Run but no RunE; keep advisory because command policy may be intentional.
